pattern: Special indices can never be adjacent, so the largest possible count is a fixed ceiling on the number of interior positions, and among all the ways to hit that ceiling there are only a handful of essentially different arrangements, letting the cheapest one be found by sliding a single 'extra gap' across the row.
hints: No two interior positions can both be special at once, so the maximum number of special indices achievable is fixed in advance from the array's length alone -- it never depends on the actual values.

For a candidate special position, only that position itself is ever worth raising, and the cost is determined completely by its two original neighbors -- raising a neighbor only makes another candidate's job harder.

When the target count exactly equals the maximum achievable, the valid arrangements are extremely constrained: they are all the same alternating pattern except for exactly one place where a single extra gap has been inserted, so computing the cost of the default pattern and then sliding that one extra gap across every possible position (updating the cost in O(1) per shift) finds the cheapest arrangement in linear time.

edge_inputs.description: a single interior position becomes special with one operation
the interior position needing the fewest operations is chosen
two special indices are achievable and both are worth raising
the smallest possible array with a single interior position
the array is already strictly alternating so it may already have the maximum special count with zero cost
flat runs of equal values mean every interior position starts tied, so choosing which ones to raise changes the total cost
an even number of interior candidate positions creates several equally maximal arrangements that differ in cost

stress.description: random small array, exhaustively cross-checkable against the brute-force oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 14))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small array with many tied values, exhaustively cross-checkable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 15))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 4) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
